title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox starter Connelly Early struggled with control, walking four batters but managed to limit the Padres to two runs.
Aroldis Chapman allowed the Padres' winning run in the ninth inning as the Red Sox lost to San Diego 3-2.
Connelly Early has tied the Red Sox record with 39 strikeouts through his first six career regular season games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0 in their second home game of the 2026 season.
Head coach Marko Mitrović was ejected in the 34th minute for leaving the technical area, according to match officials.
Peyton Miller secured the win with a late goal, extending the team's home record to 2-0 in 2026.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo’s injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas' rib injury doesn't appear long-term; he'll resume hitting from a tee after initial intercostal strain concerns.
Johan Oviedo faces a possible setback, meeting his Tommy John surgeon due to a right elbow strain and velocity dip.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval are nearing rehab outings after missing major league games since 2024.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, 22, a cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor, diagnosed with a rare thyroid cancer at 19, received life-saving treatment at Mass General Hospital.
She credits MGH and a survivorship class for support and plans to run in gratitude to her doctors and peers.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Team BMC.
She aims to raise $10,000 supporting Boston Medical Center's social workers and safety-net mission.
Her inspiration comes from her experiences with hospice social work and her roommate's work at BMC.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, extending their search for a first league win.
Coach Filipa Patão highlighted Boston's struggles in the final third, emphasizing the need for patience and accurate passing.
Star forward Amanda Gutierres, with 40 touches, struggled to connect passes effectively and has yet to score this season.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended Melrose attorney, charged with embezzlement, larceny, and unauthorized law practice.
Prosecutors allege Rappa misused client funds exceeding $660,000 and violated court orders to stop practicing law.
Rappa's bail set at $2,500; ordered to surrender passport and avoid witness contact pending trial.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning car after crashing into the Bedford Toll Plaza in New Hampshire.
Mirman thanked rescuers and hospital staff in a social media post, expressing gratitude and humor following the crash.
The incident is under investigation with no charges filed; officials seek information from witnesses.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, charged after assaulting a NH State Trooper in Dover, NH on Thursday.
The incident occurred at a parking lot off Dover Point Road as the trooper was in his cruiser.
Charges include felony criminal threatening, DWI, and attempting to take a firearm from law enforcement.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, runs the Boston Marathon for Mass General in memory of Sharla Sieve, his 'second mother,' who died from cancer.
Mallett honors Sharla Sieve's memory by supporting hepatic arterial infusion programs, after her fundraiser raised over $123,000.
Mallett, who became a videographer for the Boston Bruins in 2024, aims to complete all major marathons, starting with Boston.
